content:
# Per-Clip White Balance — Decision Record (2026-04-01)
## What: Phase 1b script estimates per-clip WB from D-Log M keyframes using filtered grey-world, applied to Resolve Node 2.
## Key Design Decisions:
1. Use dlog_m_to_linear() NOT eotf_sRGB — keyframes from Phase 1 are D-Log M encoded (ffmpeg does no color space conversion). 5-persona review caught this critical bug.
2. Saturation threshold = 0.92 in linear space — underwater illuminant has ~0.85 saturation due to extreme R:B ratio. Lower thresholds (0.70) filter out the very pixels we need.
3. D-Log M luminance thresholds: MIN=0.02, MAX=0.60 — D-Log M middle grey decodes to ~0.18 linear; highlights rarely exceed 0.6.
4. GAIN_MAX = 3.5 — Beer-Lambert says 5m depth needs 3.67x red correction; LUT provides ~2.0x static, so Node 2 needs up to 1.8x residual.
5. Gain normalization to min=1.0 — boost-only strategy avoids the gain-normalization bug from issue #3.
## Review Findings Worth Remembering:
- Grey-world assumption fails for underwater macro/reef scenes dominated by single-color subjects. Confidence metric is the safety valve.
- Mixed camera support (DJI D-Log M + Insta360 sRGB) needs per-clip EOTF selection in future.
## What Blocked / Surprised:
- sRGB vs D-Log M linearization was not caught during implementation — caught by Color Science reviewer. Always match EOTF to source encoding.
